Arizona · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Department of Health Services — Speech-Language Pathology under the Arizona Department of Health Services (ADHS). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$150
$100 application + $50 license fee
Renewal
$100
Every 2 years
Processing Time
2 to 4 weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Department of Health Services — Speech-Language Pathology

Mailing Address

150 N. 18th Ave., Suite 460, Phoenix, AZ 85007

Physical Address

150 N. 18th Ave., Suite 460, Phoenix, AZ 85007

Phone & Fax

Phone: (602) 364-2079

Fax: (602) 364-4769

Email

speechpathology@azdhs.gov

Credential Categories

Arizona SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist

The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Arizona.

Temporary SLP License

Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship. Allows supervised practice in Arizona.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Arizona Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331, score 162).

Complete Clinical Fellowship

Complete 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours) under an ASHA-certified SLP.

Submit Application to ADHS

Submit the application with the $100 fee plus $50 license fee. Include transcripts, Praxis scores, and CF verification.

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF completion documentation.
  • Fingerprint clearance cardArizona DPS fingerprint clearance card required.
Telepractice

Arizona Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Arizona permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. The compact allows SLPs licensed in member states to practice across state lines through a compact privilege.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Arizona issues a Temporary SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship, allowing supervised practice.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Arizona is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Arizona through a compact privilege. Traditional endorsement pathways are also available for non-compact applicants.

Frequently Asked Questions

Arizona SLP FAQ

How much does an Arizona SLP license cost?

The total initial cost is approximately $150. Biennial renewal is $100.

Is Arizona part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

Yes. Arizona has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

Does Arizona allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes. Arizona permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.
